While Gland creates a fixed-size feature grid, the objective is to generate the geometry of arbitrary size for long-distance camera motion.
A way is devised to extend the feature grid in the x and z dimensions to enable long-distance camera motion at inference time.
Sampling noise codes z in a grid arrangement generates a 2D layout feature grid of size H × W.
The image interpolation approach from SOAT is generalized to two dimensions for a smooth transition between independently sampled layout features.
The modulated feature fk, l+1 is constructed for each of the four corner anchors k by applying Gl, the l-th layer of Gland, in a fully convolutional manner over the entire sub-grid.

The interpolation weights βk(x, z) are used to interpolate between the four feature grids by stitching 2 x 2 sub-grids in an overlapping manner, resulting in a scene layout feature grid of arbitrary size.
Due to the computational cost of volume rendering, a reﬁnement network Gup is used to upsample the initial generated image ILR to a higher-resolution result IHR, while adding textural details.
The reﬁnement network Gup uses StyleGAN2 backbone to produce a reﬁned terrain image IHR, by replacing feature layers with feature output, and concatenating RGB residual layers with ILR, dLR, and mLR.
The reﬁnement network also predicts a reﬁned disparity map and sky mask for compositing with the skydome.
A reconstruction loss is computed between the initial and reﬁned disparity and mask outputs, and Gup is penalized for producing gray sky pixels in IHR outside the predicted mask mHR.
For fine texture details, StyleGAN2 also uses layer-wise techniques to enhance the overall quality of the image.
StyleGAN2 uses layer-wise spatial noise in intermediate generator layers for fine texture details.
Using a fixed 2D noise pattern results in texture 'sticking' as we move the camera.
Removing the 2D noise pattern entirely results in convolutional gridding artifacts.
To improve spatial consistency, the 2D image-space noise is replaced with projected 3D world-space noise.
